title: Talking to the building manager
description: Report a maintenance issue to the 大廈管理員
location: Residential building management office, North Point
context: Every residential building in Hong Kong has a management office (管理處) and a 管理員 (guard/manager) on duty around the clock. They handle maintenance requests, parcel collection, and building rules. It helps to be polite and specific about your issue.
cultural_note: Hong Kong's Owners' Corporations (業主立案法團) are mini-governments that manage buildings, collect fees, and handle disputes between residents. In older tenement buildings (唐樓, tong4 lau4), the management office is often a single uncle at a folding table in the lobby who has been there for thirty years and knows everything about everyone.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 唔該，我係三零六室嘅住客。我想報告一個問題。
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, ngo5 hai6 saam1 ling4 luk6 sat1 ge3 zyu6 haak3. ngo5 soeng2 bou3 gou3 jat1 go3 man6 tai4.
lines.english: Excuse me, I'm a resident of flat 306. I'd like to report a problem.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: zau2 long4 zaan2 dang1 waai6 zo2, hak1 saai3.
lines.english: The corridor light is broken — it's completely dark.

lines.speaker: manager
lines.jyutping: hai6 gei2 lau4 zau2 long4 ga3?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 係三樓，電梯出嚟轉右個走廊。
lines.jyutping: hai6 saam1 lau4, din6 tai1 ceot1 lei4 zyun3 jau6 go3 zau2 long4.
lines.english: Third floor, the corridor to the right when you exit the lift.

lines.speaker: manager
lines.cjk: 明白，我幫你記低，即刻叫人去睇。
lines.jyutping: ming4 baak6, ngo5 bong1 nei5 gei3 dai1, zik1 hak1 giu3 jan4 heoi3 tai2.
lines.english: Got it, I'll make a note and send someone to take a look right away.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 好，唔該晒。大概幾時整好呀？
lines.jyutping: hou2, m4 goi1 saai3. daai6 koi3 gei2 si4 zing2 hou2 aa3?
lines.english: Great, thank you. Roughly when will it be fixed?

lines.speaker: manager
lines.jyutping: gam1 jat6 haa6 zau3 jing1 goi1 dak1.
